Real `Player` ownership storage, item object registry and packet serializers remain #NEXT.R8.ENTITIES.013/#008.

This matches the represented branches of `LootItem::AllowedForPlayer` -> `Player::HasQuestForItem`; rows remain hidden when the player lacks the represented quest objective/drop allowance. Represented `Rate.Drop.*` modifiers are closed by #NEXT.R8.ENTITIES.190.

Unsupported reference conditions, non-player condition targets, script-backed conditions and unsupported condition types fail closed rather than granting loot. This is not the full reusable `ConditionMgr`; canonical conditions for non-item loot, phasing, SmartAI, gossip, vendors and scripts remain pending.

Generated rows compute `freeforall` from `ITEM_FLAG_MULTI_DROP`, compute `follow_loot_rules` like Trinity's constructor as `!needs_quest || ITEM_FLAGS_CU_FOLLOW_LOOT_RULES`, and initialize blocked/counted/under-threshold to false. This closes represented metadata/persistence parity only; actual group/master/NBG roll routing, threshold blocking and per-player FFA partitioning remain pending under the canonical loot work.

Represented full-looted checks treat FFA rows as complete only when every represented allowed looter has looted that row. This intentionally remains represented/session-local: canonical `Cell::VisitGridObjects`, full `Player::isAllowedToLoot`, shared loot ownership, group/fanout/criteria/script hooks, `SMSG_LOOT_RELEASE_ALL` and config hot reload remain pending.

  Rust targets: `crates/wow-world/src/handlers/loot.rs`, docs in this miniphase and `docs/migration/loot.md`.

The existing represented loot open flag now tracks C++ `_wasOpened`, so later opens do not resend the candidate list. Unit coverage verifies packet order, candidate GUID payload and first-open-only behavior. Full canonical shared `Loot` ownership, `Loot::NotifyLootList`, roll creation and cross-session candidate/fanout state remain pending.

- [x] **#NEXT.R8.ENTITIES.245** Represent `Loot::NotifyLootList` fanout from current represented loot state.
  C++ refs: `/home/server/woltk-trinity-legacy/src/server/game/Loot/Loot.cpp` (`Loot::NotifyLootList`, `Loot::hasOverThresholdItem`), `/home/server/woltk-trinity-legacy/src/server/game/Entities/Unit/Unit.cpp` (`loot->NotifyLootList(creature->GetMap())` after group creature-loot creation).
  Rust targets: `crates/wow-world/src/handlers/loot.rs`, docs in this miniphase and `docs/migration/loot.md`.

This is intentionally hooked to represented open-time state until the canonical creature-death/group loot creation hook from C++ `Unit.cpp` exists.

- [x] **#NEXT.R8.ENTITIES.246** Represent first-open group-loot roll start prompts.
  C++ refs: `/home/server/woltk-trinity-legacy/src/server/game/Loot/Loot.cpp` (`Loot::OnLootOpened`, `LootRoll::TryToStart`, `LootRoll::SendStartRoll`), `/home/server/woltk-trinity-legacy/src/server/game/Loot/Loot.h` (`LOOT_ROLL_TIMEOUT`, roll masks and slot types).
  Rust targets: `crates/wow-world/src/handlers/loot.rs`, `docs/migration/inventory/r3-opcodes-registry.{tsv,md}`, docs in this miniphase and `docs/migration/loot.md`.
  Acceptance: represented first-open handling now mirrors the C++ `_wasOpened` split: after `SMSG_LOOT_RESPONSE` and represented `SMSG_LOOT_LIST`, `MASTER_LOOT` sends the existing candidate list path while `GROUP_LOOT`/`NEED_BEFORE_GREED` starts represented `SMSG_START_LOOT_ROLL` prompts for connected allowed looters when a blocked item has more than one connected candidate. The prompt uses the C++ 60s timeout, represented no-disenchant roll mask, roll-ongoing loot slot UI type, loot object, map and loot method; if only one connected candidate exists, the item is marked under-threshold and unblocked like `LootRoll::TryToStart`. Current-session `pass_on_group_loot` suppresses the local prompt while still counting the player for roll start eligibility. Canonical persisted `LootRoll` state, timers, votes, winner resolution, need/disenchant eligibility and completion fanout remain pending.

- [x] **#NEXT.R8.ENTITIES.247** Represent immediate `LootRoll::PlayerVote` broadcast for current-session group rolls.
  C++ refs: `/home/server/woltk-trinity-legacy/src/server/game/Handlers/LootHandler.cpp` (`HandleLootRoll`), `/home/server/woltk-trinity-legacy/src/server/game/Loot/Loot.cpp` (`LootRoll::PlayerVote`, `LootRoll::SendRoll`).
  Rust targets: `crates/wow-world/src/handlers/loot.rs`, `docs/migration/inventory/r3-opcodes-registry.{tsv,md}`, docs in this miniphase and `docs/migration/loot.md`.
  Acceptance: represented `CMSG_LOOT_ROLL` now resolves the active represented loot object, requires a blocked `GROUP_LOOT`/`NEED_BEFORE_GREED` item where the current player is an allowed looter, ignores current-session `pass_on_group_loot` like C++ players without an attached roll, and broadcasts the immediate `SMSG_LOOT_ROLL` packet to connected allowed looters on the same map. The represented immediate C++ roll values are preserved: Pass/Greed/Disenchant send `Roll=-1`, Need sends `Roll=0`, and unsupported roll types return silently. Unit coverage verifies a Need vote broadcast reaches both the voter and a connected allowed looter. Persistent shared `LootRoll` state, random 1..100 stored rolls, all-voted/timer finish, winner selection, `SMSG_LOOT_ROLL_WON`, `SMSG_LOOT_ALL_PASSED`, item storage and cross-session remote voting remain pending.

- [x] **#NEXT.R8.ENTITIES.248** Represent all-voted group-roll winner finish for current-session shared state.
  C++ refs: `/home/server/woltk-trinity-legacy/src/server/game/Loot/Loot.cpp` (`LootRoll::AllPlayerVoted`, `LootRoll::Finish`, `LootRoll::SendLootRollWon`), `/home/server/woltk-trinity-legacy/src/server/game/Loot/Loot.h` (`PlayerRollVote`, `RollVoteMap`).
  Rust targets: `crates/wow-world/src/session.rs`, `crates/wow-world/src/handlers/loot.rs`, docs in this miniphase and `docs/migration/loot.md`.
  Acceptance: represented `WorldSession` now keeps pending group/NBG roll state keyed by `(LootObj, LootListID)` when first-open roll prompts are started. Valid current-session votes update that state, assign a represented 1..100 roll number for Need/Greed/Disenchant, and when every represented voter has voted, the same C++ winner priority is applied: Need beats Greed/Disenchant, otherwise highest Greed/Disenchant roll wins, Pass cannot win. Finishing unblocks the loot item, stores `roll_winner`, removes the pending roll, and sends `SMSG_LOOT_ROLL_WON` with locked UI to non-winners and allow-loot UI to the winner. Unit coverage simulates two voters over one represented shared state and verifies Need beats Greed, the item is unblocked, winner GUID is stored and both packet variants are emitted. Remaining gaps: real cross-session shared roll ownership, command routing for remote `CMSG_LOOT_ROLL`, C++ `urand(1,100)` source, timer expiry, all-passed exact branch, criteria updates and winner item storage.

- [x] **#NEXT.R8.ENTITIES.249** Match C++ all-passed group-roll finish fanout for represented valid voters.
  C++ refs: `/home/server/woltk-trinity-legacy/src/server/game/Loot/Loot.cpp` (`LootRoll::SendAllPassed`, `LootRoll::Finish`).
  Rust targets: `crates/wow-world/src/handlers/loot.rs`, docs in this miniphase and `docs/migration/loot.md`.
  Acceptance: represented all-pass finish now follows the contrasted C++ branch instead of intuitive broadcast semantics: when all valid represented voters choose Pass, the roll item is unblocked, no `roll_winner` is set, pending roll state is removed, and `SMSG_LOOT_ALL_PASSED` is sent only to voters with `RollVote::NotValid` in the represented roll map. The common all-valid-voters-pass case therefore emits only the per-player `SMSG_LOOT_ROLL` pass broadcasts and no extra all-passed packet, matching this Trinity branch. Unit coverage verifies two valid represented voters passing closes the roll without any `SMSG_LOOT_ALL_PASSED` fanout. Remaining gaps: modelling invalid voters from disconnected/ineligible allowed looters, timer expiry, cross-session vote ownership and winner item storage.

- [x] **#NEXT.R8.ENTITIES.250** Add represented owner-session command path for remote group-roll votes.
  C++ refs: `/home/server/woltk-trinity-legacy/src/server/game/Handlers/LootHandler.cpp` (`HandleLootRoll`), `/home/server/woltk-trinity-legacy/src/server/game/Loot/Loot.cpp` (`LootRoll::PlayerVote`, `LootRoll::SendRoll`, `LootRoll::AllPlayerVoted`).
  Rust targets: `crates/wow-network/src/player_registry.rs`, `crates/wow-network/src/lib.rs`, `crates/wow-world/src/handlers/loot.rs`, docs in this miniphase and `docs/migration/loot.md`.
  Acceptance: represented cross-session roll mutation now has the same ownership direction as C++ `Player::GetLootRoll(...)->PlayerVote(...)`: `SessionCommand::LootRollVote` carries voter GUID, loot object, loot-list id, roll type and the voter's pass-on-group-loot flag to the owner session, and the owner session applies it to its pending represented roll state using the same immediate `SMSG_LOOT_ROLL` fanout/update path as local votes. Unit coverage verifies a connected remote greed vote command updates the owner session's roll state and broadcasts the C++ immediate roll packet to both local and remote allowed looters. Remaining gaps: actual remote `CMSG_LOOT_ROLL` discovery/routing to the owner session, canonical shared `Loot`/`LootRoll` ownership, timer expiry, C++ RNG source and winner item storage.

- [x] **#NEXT.R8.ENTITIES.251** Route represented remote `CMSG_LOOT_ROLL` to the owner session.
  C++ refs: `/home/server/woltk-trinity-legacy/src/server/game/Handlers/LootHandler.cpp` (`HandleLootRoll`), `/home/server/woltk-trinity-legacy/src/server/game/Loot/Loot.cpp` (`LootRoll::PlayerVote`).
  Rust targets: `crates/wow-network/src/player_registry.rs`, `crates/wow-world/src/session.rs`, `crates/wow-world/src/handlers/loot.rs`, docs in this miniphase and `docs/migration/loot.md`.
  Acceptance: the represented owner session now publishes pending `(LootObj, LootListID)` keys through `PlayerBroadcastInfo::active_loot_rolls` whenever first-open roll state is created or finished. If a remote session receives `CMSG_LOOT_ROLL` and has no local roll state, it searches same-map registered sessions for the pending key and sends `SessionCommand::LootRollVote` to that owner's command channel, preserving the voter's pass-on-group-loot flag. Unit coverage simulates two sessions and verifies the remote handler routes a Greed vote to the owner session, then the owner emits the C++ immediate `SMSG_LOOT_ROLL` fanout and records the remote vote. Remaining gaps: canonical shared `Loot`/`LootRoll` ownership, timer expiry, C++ RNG source, disconnected/invalid voter modelling and winner item storage.

- [x] **#NEXT.R8.ENTITIES.252** Represent `LootRoll::UpdateRoll` timeout finish.
  C++ refs: `/home/server/woltk-trinity-legacy/src/server/game/Loot/Loot.cpp` (`LootRoll::UpdateRoll`, `LootRoll::AllPlayerVoted`, `LootRoll::Finish`), `/home/server/woltk-trinity-legacy/src/server/game/Loot/Loot.h` (`m_endTime`).
  Rust targets: `crates/wow-world/src/session.rs`, `crates/wow-world/src/handlers/loot.rs`, docs in this miniphase and `docs/migration/loot.md`.
  Acceptance: represented pending roll state now stores an `end_time` matching the C++ 60s `LOOT_ROLL_TIMEOUT`; the session update loop ticks represented rolls and, when a roll expires, finishes it using the current Need-over-Greed winner without requiring every voter to have voted, mirroring `UpdateRoll`'s `AllPlayerVoted(winnerItr) || m_endTime <= GameTime::Now()` branch. Expiry unblocks the item, stores `roll_winner` when a winner exists, removes the pending roll and republishes owner-session roll keys. Unit coverage forces an expired roll with one Greed vote and one pending voter and verifies the current Greed voter wins. Remaining gaps: C++ `SendLootRollWon`'s final per-voter `SMSG_LOOT_ROLL` value replay before `SMSG_LOOT_ROLL_WON`, canonical shared `Loot`/`LootRoll` ownership, C++ RNG source, disconnected/invalid voter modelling and winner item storage.

- [x] **#NEXT.R8.ENTITIES.253** Replay final `LootRoll::SendLootRollWon` vote values.

Remaining gaps: canonical shared `Loot`/`LootRoll` ownership, C++ RNG source, disconnected/invalid voter modelling, criteria updates and winner item storage.

- [x] **#NEXT.R8.ENTITIES.256** Model represented `LootRoll` startup `NotValid`/auto-pass voters.
  C++ refs: `/home/server/woltk-trinity-legacy/src/server/game/Loot/Loot.cpp` (`LootRoll::TryToStart`, `LootRoll::SendStartRoll`, `LootRoll::SendRoll`, `LootRoll::SendAllPassed`).

  Acceptance: represented `LootRoll` startup now builds the vote map from every allowed looter like C++: connected same-map voters become `NotEmittedYet` or `Pass` based on `pass_on_group_loot`, while disconnected/off-map allowed looters become `NotValid`. `SMSG_START_LOOT_ROLL` is only sent to `NotEmittedYet` voters, and `Pass` voters emit the C++ auto-pass `SMSG_LOOT_ROLL` fanout. Unit coverage verifies a disconnected allowed looter is retained as `NotValid` and pass-on-loot suppresses the prompt while broadcasting the pass roll. Remaining gaps: canonical shared `Loot`/`LootRoll` ownership, real `ConditionMgr`/`AllowedForPlayer` invalid-voter checks, real achievements/criteria backend integration and winner item storage.

- [x] **#NEXT.R8.ENTITIES.257** Store represented non-disenchant `LootRoll` winner items.
  C++ refs: `/home/server/woltk-trinity-legacy/src/server/game/Loot/Loot.cpp` (`LootRoll::Finish`, `Player::StoreLootItem`).

A current-session winner calls the existing direct represented `StoreLootItem` bridge and removes the represented slot only after a successful store. A connected remote winner is routed through a bounded `SessionCommand::LootRollStoreWinner` so the target session runs its own allowed-looter, roll-winner and `CanStoreItem`/store checks before the owner marks/removes the slot. Timer expiry was moved from the sync update loop into async `process_pending` so this finish path can await DB/target-session work. Remaining gaps: canonical shared `Loot`/`LootRoll` ownership, live DB-backed successful remote assignment validation, disenchant winner `AutoStore`/retrieval-mail fallback, real `ConditionMgr`/`AllowedForPlayer` invalid-voter checks and real achievements/criteria backend integration.

The shared `condition_compare_values_like_cpp` follows the contrasted C++ `ComparisionType` order (`0 ==`, `1 >`, `2 <`, `3 >=`, `4 <=`) and returns `None` for unsupported values, fixing the creature-loot level-comparison mapping introduced in the local bridge. Remaining gaps: full canonical `ConditionMgr` object/source storage, condition-reference expansion, script-backed/non-player-target conditions and non-loot condition consumers.

The item-loot bridge and represented creature corpse bridge both preload nested condition-reference templates for direct and referenced loot rows. One brittle Rust-only test assumption was fixed after C++ contrast: `CurrencyTypesStore` now tests lookup semantics against a real loaded DB2 ID instead of requiring ID `395`, because C++ global stores require correct `HasRecord`/`LookupEntry` behavior over available records, not that a specific local client-data row exists. This checkpoint does not certify full port parity; it only establishes a build/test-clean baseline for the next C++-contrasted gap closures.

Represented `CMSG_OPEN_ITEM` item money loot now consumes the shared helper instead of a private duplicate, and represented creature corpse money now applies `Rate.Drop.Money` to DB gold bounds instead of ignoring the configured rate. Remaining gaps: full canonical `Loot` ownership/state, money generation stored on canonical `Loot`, and removal of the represented legacy fallback for creatures without DB gold bounds.

## Follow-Up Work Items

- [ ] **#NEXT.R8.ENTITIES.003** Bind `wow-map` grid unload actions to real entity methods once Creature/GameObject/Corpse exist.
  Rust targets: `crates/wow-entities/src/creature.rs`, `crates/wow-entities/src/game_object.rs`, `crates/wow-entities/src/corpse.rs`, `crates/wow-map/src/grid_unload.rs`.
  Acceptance: `wow-map` keeps the GUID-only unload collector unchanged and adds a `GridUnloadEntityStore` applier for real `Creature`/`GameObject`/`Corpse` instances; `SetDestroyedObject` now calls entity-level destroyed-object methods, `CleanupsBeforeDelete` records represented cleanup calls, `DeleteObject` records represented delete requests and clears grid/cell state, creature `CombatStop` clears the represented attack target, creature/gameobject respawn relocation requests are recorded, and currently unrepresented creature dyn-object/area-trigger ownership is an explicit no-op. #NEXT.R8.ENTITIES.417 connects that trait to canonical `Map::map_objects`, but only as a map-owned bridge; live grid unload, remove-list execution, cross-session fanout and full lifecycle cleanup remain pending under #NEXT.R8.ENTITIES.006/#NEXT.R8.ENTITIES.017/#NEXT.R8.ENTITIES.021/#NEXT.R8.ENTITIES.023 and the map runtime work.
- [ ] **#NEXT.R8.ENTITIES.005** Port `WorldObject` LOS, terrain-height, transport relocation and visibility-range helpers that require Map/Terrain/Transport integration.
  Note: foundation progress recorded only as a bridge-level port. `IsWithinLOSInMap` now matches the represented C++ prefilter (`IsInMap`) and exposes source/target endpoint metadata, but canonical collision-height and `GetHitSpherePointFor(...)` endpoint adjustment remain TODO until RustyCore has shared collision/hit-sphere infrastructure.
- [ ] **#NEXT.R8.ENTITIES.006** Complete remaining `ObjectAccessor` integration: real `SaveAllPlayers` and wiring to canonical `wow_map::Map` containers instead of bridge storage.
  Note: foundation progress recorded at the minimal canonical-storage bridge level. `ObjectAccessor::save_all_players_with` now executes a real save callback per registered player and reports the failing player GUID; `wow-map::Map` owns a validated `MapObjectRecord` store and implements `ObjectAccessorMapSource` so ObjectAccessor lookups can read Map-backed records. Full typed `Map` containers/ownership for `Creature`, `GameObject`, etc. remain deferred to the entity lifecycle tasks.

This is not a full runtime inventory system yet: real packet serializers/delivery beyond the represented bridges, complete character/item persistence and recursive bag/bank/buyback save/load, map/world ownership integration, collection appearance registration, full stat/aura/spell application and remaining runtime side effects stay deferred to follow-up packet/persistence/world/gameplay tasks.

Real DB loaders, packet serializers/delivery, full AchievementMgr/ReputationMgr/SocialMgr/Mail managers, spell/aura runtime, group/guild/BG queue behavior and world/session integration remain deferred.

Real ObjectMgr/DB loaders, real Map insertion/duplicate store, phase/terrain/collision, addon/equipment DB details, AI/threat runtime, respawn group/pool semantics and full update packet/runtime behavior remain deferred to #NEXT.R8.ENTITIES.017 or other follow-up tasks.

  Note: foundation progress recorded as Creature runtime lifecycle bridge/foundation; real AI execution, ThreatManager, Map linked respawn/spawn group/pool, loot object contents, reputation reward formulas, pickpocket loot refill DB, packet visibility delivery, and full world-session runtime integration remain deferred.
- [ ] **#NEXT.R8.ENTITIES.018** Move real AI ownership from `wow-ai`/`wow-world::WorldCreature` bridge into canonical `Creature`/Map refs without mixing entity state into session.
  Note: foundation progress recorded as an ownership foundation. `wow-entities::Creature` now owns the represented AI runtime/ownership state (state machine, home/move target/timers, combat target, swing/aggro/wander/respawn/corpse metadata, display/faction/npc/unit flags and damage bounds) without depending on `wow-ai`/`wow-world`; `wow-world::WorldCreature` wraps canonical `Creature` as its source of truth and MapManager add/get/visibility reads derive GUID/position/health/runtime from it. `WorldSession` spawn/respawn registration can insert canonical map creatures when a shared MapManager exists, but it intentionally keeps the legacy per-session `CreatureAI` fallback/cache until the broader session migration lands. Deferred: real AI scripting, ThreatManager, complete packet movement/health updates, DB respawn persistence, grid relocation across cells and full removal of the session legacy `HashMap`.
- [ ] **#NEXT.R8.ENTITIES.020** Complete `GameObject` create/load/template lifecycle: `GameObject::Create`, `CreateGameObjectFromDB`, template/addon refs, rotations, model/collision creation, spawn data, map insertion and respawn compatibility.
- [ ] **#NEXT.R8.ENTITIES.021** Complete `GameObject` runtime lifecycle: update loop, loot/use state machine, door/button/trap/chest/fishing/destructible behavior, cooldown/restock, despawn/respawn persistence and grid unload bindings.
  Note: foundation progress recorded for the `CMSG_GAME_OBJ_USE` remote-control guard. `GameObjectTemplateData::is_usable_mounted_like_cpp` now mirrors C++ `GameObjectTemplate::IsUsableMounted`, and represented gameobject use rejects a remote-controlled mover unless the player is on a vehicle, mounted, or the target template allows mounted use. Remaining gaps: full `GameObject::Use` dispatch, real vehicle passenger ownership, GameObjectAI/script hooks, ReportUse `OnReportUse`, persistent criteria manager and canonical GO runtime ownership.
  Note: foundation progress recorded for the `CMSG_GAME_OBJ_REPORT_USE` AI gate. Represented report-use now records the C++ `go->AI()->OnReportUse(_player)` hook and suppresses represented `UseGameobject` criteria when the represented AI result is `true`, matching the C++ early return without pretending to execute SmartAI scripts. Remaining gaps: real `GameObjectAI` ownership, SmartAI `SMART_EVENT_GOSSIP_HELLO` execution, persistent criteria manager and canonical GO runtime ownership.
  Note: foundation progress recorded for the player preamble of `GameObject::Use`. `GameObjectTemplateData::get_no_damage_immune_like_cpp` now mirrors C++ `GetNoDamageImmune`, represented use rejects immune players when required, removes represented mounted state when the template is not usable mounted, records `PlayerTalkClass->ClearMenus`, and gates dispatch through represented `AI()->OnGossipHello`. Remaining gaps: real aura removal by type across canonical aura ownership, real PlayerTalkClass/GossipMenu state, SmartAI gossip execution, cooldown persistence and the remaining per-type `GameObject::Use` branches.
  Note: foundation progress recorded for the `GameObject::Use` template cooldown gate. `GameObjectTemplateData::get_cooldown_like_cpp` now mirrors the C++ `GetCooldown` switch for trap/goober, represented use starts/rejects session-local cooldowns before type dispatch like C++, and represented trap use applies the C++ 4-second fallback when template cooldown is zero. Remaining gaps: canonical `GameObject::m_cooldownTime`, wall-clock persistence across map ownership and full trap/goober spell execution.
  Note: foundation progress recorded for the door/button `GameObject::Use` branch. `GameObjectTemplateData::get_auto_close_time_like_cpp` now mirrors the representable C++ `GetAutoCloseTime` subset, and represented door/button use applies the C++ ready-state gate, toggles represented GO state, sets `GO_FLAG_IN_USE`, sets `GO_ACTIVATED` with the user GUID, stores the restore cooldown and wires the represented update tick into session pending processing for activated door/button objects, clearing `GO_FLAG_IN_USE`, restoring the previous GO state and moving to `GO_JUST_DEACTIVATED` when the cooldown expires. Remaining gaps: real UpdateObject fanout, collision/model updates and canonical `GameObject::Update` ownership.
  Note: foundation progress recorded for the trap `GameObject::Use` branch. `GameObjectTemplateData::trap_use_source_like_cpp` now captures C++ trap radius/spell/charges/cooldown/startDelay/IgnoreTotems/Checkallunits fields, represented trap use records the spell cast, applies the C++ cooldown fallback of 4 seconds when template cooldown is zero, rejects active cooldowns, and moves one-charge traps to `GO_JUST_DEACTIVATED`. Session pending processing also represents the C++ trap `GO_NOT_READY` path: bomb traps (`trap.charges == 2`) arm to `GO_READY` with the hardcoded 10s cooldown, non-bomb traps move to `GO_READY`, and non-bomb traps with represented owner-in-combat state arm `trap.startDelay`; expired bomb `GO_READY` moves to `GO_ACTIVATED`, and `GO_ACTIVATED` records the null-target bomb spell hook before `GO_JUST_DEACTIVATED`. Non-bomb traps now also mirror the C++ `GO_READY` target activation and `GO_ACTIVATED` trigger path: environmental traps use radius/2 against the current represented player, owner/check-all-units traps can consume an explicit represented target GUID, target GUID is stored as `lootStateUnitGUID`, activated traps record target spell casts with `OriginalCaster`, arm template-or-4s cooldown and apply the C++ charges split. Remaining gaps: real `GameObject::CastSpell`, spell validation/outdoor-PvP fallback, canonical cooldown ownership, real ObjectAccessor target search/hostility filtering, canonical owner combat state, LOS/totem filtering and real UpdateObject/despawn processing.
  Note: foundation progress recorded for the spell-focus `GameObject::Use` branch. `GameObjectTemplateData::spell_focus_linked_trap_like_cpp` now mirrors C++ `spellFocus.linkedTrap` (`Data2`), and represented spell-focus use records the `TriggeringLinkedGameObject` hook only when the linked trap entry is nonzero. Remaining gaps: real linked-trap lookup/spawn/cast semantics and canonical `GameObject::Use` script/runtime ownership.
  Note: foundation progress recorded for the camera `GameObject::Use` branch. `GameObjectTemplateData::camera_use_source_like_cpp` now mirrors C++ `camera.camera` (`Data1`) and `camera.eventID` (`Data2`), sends the C++ `SMSG_TRIGGER_CINEMATIC` packet with an empty conversation GUID, and represented camera use records the cinematic-start and `GameEvents::Trigger` hook points when nonzero. Remaining gaps: real cinematic manager state, GameEvents execution and canonical script/runtime ownership.
  Note: foundation progress recorded for the first goober `GameObject::Use` sub-branch. `GameObjectTemplateData::goober_use_source_like_cpp` now captures the C++ goober data indices for quest/event/page/spell/linked-trap/gossip/multi-interact/player-cast, represented goober use sends the C++ `SMSG_PAGE_TEXT` gameobject GUID packet when `goober.pageID` is nonzero, `CMSG_QUERY_PAGE_TEXT` reads the C++ page-id/raw-item-guid layout and responds with the C++ `SMSG_QUERY_PAGE_TEXT_RESPONSE` page chain from `page_text`/`page_text_locale`, and represented goober use records page/gossip, event, quest-incomplete gating, group-distance `KillCreditGO`, and linked-trap hook points in C++ order. Remaining gaps: real gossip menu packet population, quest objective mutation and canonical GameEvents/trap execution.
  Note: foundation progress recorded for the second goober `GameObject::Use` sub-branch. Represented goober use now applies the C++ `AllowMultiInteract` split, including per-player despawn for consumable multi-interact and per-player active state otherwise; the normal branch records `GO_FLAG_IN_USE`, `GO_ACTIVATED`, sends the C++ `SMSG_GAME_OBJECT_CUSTOM_ANIM` packet using represented `GetGoAnimProgress()` when `goober.customAnim` is set, applies `GO_STATE_ACTIVE` otherwise, records autoclose timing and preserves post-switch spell caster selection from `playerCast`. Remaining gaps: real nearby-set fanout for custom animation/per-player update packets, real spell validation/execution and canonical `m_cooldownTime`/flag fanout.
  Note: foundation progress recorded for represented goober `GO_JUST_DEACTIVATED` cleanup. Represented cleanup now records linked-trap despawn, casts the goober spell to represented `m_unique_users`, clears unique users/use state, removes `GO_FLAG_IN_USE`, resets `GO_STATE_READY` when C++ would (`lockId || autoClose`), and returns non-consumable goobers to `GO_READY` while consumable goobers stay `GO_NOT_READY`. Session pending processing now also mirrors the two-step C++ update path for normal activated goobers: expired autoclose first removes `GO_FLAG_IN_USE` and moves to `GO_JUST_DEACTIVATED`, then the next represented update applies the cleanup source. Remaining gaps: real linked-trap object despawn, real `ObjectAccessor` player lookup for unique users, real spell execution, `ClearLoot` ownership and visibility/respawn/delete fanout.
  Note: foundation progress recorded for generic represented `GO_JUST_DEACTIVATED` cleanup. Non-goober represented gameobjects now clear represented loot, reset `lootStateUnitGUID` and `use_count`, move to `GO_NOT_READY`, and owner/summoned represented objects delete through represented despawn/destroy packets like the C++ `Delete()` branch. Represented `respawnDelayTime` now follows the C++ spawned-by-default split: spawned objects leave represented visibility and return to `GO_READY`/`GO_STATE_READY` when the timer expires, while non-spawned represented objects delete instead of scheduling respawn. Remaining gaps: real linked-trap lookup/despawn, GameObjectOverride flag restoration, `IsDespawnAtAction`/`goAnimProgress` despawn visuals, dynamic respawn scaling, `SaveRespawnTime`, respawnCompatibilityMode visibility destroy, map remove-list ownership and canonical map removal.
  Note: #NEXT.R8.ENTITIES.347 implementation complete for represented linked trap despawn hook in non-goober `GO_JUST_DEACTIVATED`. C++ anchors: `/home/server/woltk-trinity-legacy/src/server/game/Entities/GameObject/GameObject.cpp:1575-1579`, `/home/server/woltk-trinity-legacy/src/server/game/Entities/GameObject/GameObject.cpp:3909-3912`, `/home/server/woltk-trinity-legacy/src/server/game/Entities/GameObject/GameObjectData.h:1049-1059`. Rust now records source/template `linked_trap_entry` in represented `WorldSession` GO state for chest and gathering-node sources and emits `GameObjectLinkedTrapDespawn` before represented clear/delete-respawn cleanup when known. This is not canonical map/ObjectAccessor despawn; residual TODO: real `m_linkedTrap` GUID ownership, `ObjectAccessor::GetGameObject`, canonical `DespawnOrUnsummon`, map removal and cross-session fanout remain pending. Fallback without known `linked_trap_entry` emits no represented effect.
  Note: #NEXT.R8.ENTITIES.348 implementation complete for a canonical GameObject-owned loot-state dependency inside `wow-entities::GameObject`. C++ anchors: `/home/server/woltk-trinity-legacy/src/server/game/Entities/GameObject/GameObject.cpp:3711-3733` and `/home/server/woltk-trinity-legacy/src/server/game/Loot/Loot.h:283-306`. Rust now owns shared loot (`m_loot`), personal loot (`m_personalLoot`), unique users (`m_unique_users`) and use-times state in the entity, with `Loot::isLooted` represented exactly as `gold == 0 && unlooted_count == 0`, plus `clear_loot_like_cpp` and `is_fully_looted_like_cpp` unit coverage for shared/personal/empty cases. This is only the autocontained canonical helper/storage slice; `WorldSession::DoLootRelease`, `OnLootRelease`, loot generation, map/ObjectAccessor lookup and cross-session packet fanout remain pending, and no represented fallback hook was added.
  Note: #NEXT.R8.ENTITIES.349 implementation complete for the represented-to-canonical GameObject owned-loot bridge in `WorldSession`. C++ anchors: `/home/server/woltk-trinity-legacy/src/server/game/Handlers/LootHandler.cpp:262-320`, `/home/server/woltk-trinity-legacy/src/server/game/Entities/GameObject/GameObject.cpp:3711-3768`, and `/home/server/woltk-trinity-legacy/src/server/game/Entities/GameObject/GameObject.cpp:1705-1708`. Represented GameObject chest/fishing/gathering/personal loot opening and release now synchronize available session-local shared/personal loot counts and money into canonical `wow-entities::GameObject` when the canonical object exists, release decisions prefer canonical `is_fully_looted_like_cpp`, and represented `AddUse`/`AddUniqueUse` branches update canonical counters without changing fallback behavior when no canonical object is present. Remaining gaps: full canonical `GameObject::m_loot` generation/ownership, remote per-player personal loot not represented in this session, cross-session fanout, real ObjectAccessor/Map lifecycle, dynamic flag packet rebuilds and complete GameObject runtime ownership.
  Note: #NEXT.R8.ENTITIES.350 implementation complete for represented `GO_JUST_DEACTIVATED` visual despawn ordering after C++ early returns. C++ anchors: `/home/server/woltk-trinity-legacy/src/server/game/Entities/GameObject/GameObjectData.h:843-850`, `/home/server/woltk-trinity-legacy/src/server/game/Entities/GameObject/GameObject.cpp:1575-1679`, and `/home/server/woltk-trinity-legacy/src/server/game/Entities/GameObject/GameObject.cpp:1766-1771`. Represented session pending processing now keeps `IsDespawnAtAction()` separate from `GetGoAnimProgress()`, so non-consumable CHEST and GOOBER objects without represented owner/summon take the C++ early-return path and do not send `SMSG_GAME_OBJECT_DESPAWN` even when `go_anim_progress > 0`; non-consumable CHEST restock sets represented `GO_NOT_READY` with `chest_restock_until`, otherwise returns to `GO_READY`, while generic objects reaching the later C++ branch still send the despawn visual on `go_animProgress > 0` and consumable CHEST keeps the despawn-at-action packet. Remaining gaps: canonical `GameObject::m_loot` ownership/generation, GameObjectOverride flag reset/restore, exact owner/spell-created provenance beyond represented `owner_guid`, `SaveRespawnTime`/map removal and cross-session fanout.
  Note: foundation progress recorded for the chair `GameObject::Use` branch. `GameObjectTemplateData::chair_use_source_like_cpp` now mirrors C++ chair slots/height/triggered-event fields, and represented chair use initializes slots, computes the nearest free slot along the orthogonal chair line using the DB template `size`, applies the represented player position and chair stand-state mutation, records the teleport/stand-state effect and triggers the optional event. Remaining gaps: real `NearTeleportTo` packet/movement flow, live occupant validation through `ObjectAccessor` and packet fanout.
  Note: foundation progress recorded for the questgiver `GameObject::Use` branch. `GameObjectTemplateData::questgiver_use_source_like_cpp` now mirrors C++ `questgiver.gossipID` (`Data3`), and represented questgiver use records the prepared-gossip delivery hook for player use. Remaining gaps: real `PrepareGossipMenu(..., true)`, real `SendPreparedGossip`, quest menu population and non-player caller rejection outside the current player-only represented handler.
  Note: foundation progress recorded for the spellcaster `GameObject::Use` branch. `GameObjectTemplateData::spellcaster_use_source_like_cpp` now mirrors C++ spell/charges/party-only fields, and represented spellcaster use validates `partyOnly` owner/raid state from typed canonical `GameObjectData::CreatedBy` when available before falling back to represented owner state, records the unconditional mounted-aura removal, `AddUse` counter increment and common post-switch spell hook. Represented session pending processing now mirrors the C++ `GameObject::Update` `GetCharges()` branch for spellcasters, resetting use count and moving to `GO_JUST_DEACTIVATED` when represented use count reaches the template charge count. Remaining gaps: live `GetOwner()` Unit object type validation beyond GUID/raid membership, real spell execution and packet/runtime fanout.
  Note: foundation progress recorded for the barber-chair `GameObject::Use` branch. `GameObjectTemplateData::barber_chair_use_source_like_cpp` now mirrors C++ chair-height, sit-anim-kit and customization-scope fields, sends the C++ `SMSG_ENABLE_BARBER_SHOP` customization-scope packet, applies the represented player position and chair stand-state mutation, and records the fallback teleport and stand-state effects. Remaining gaps: real `TeleportTo` flags, sit-anim-kit application to canonical unit fields and packet fanout.

### #NEXT.R8.ENTITIES.352 — Map dynamic respawn scaling helper dependency

Status: complete (helper dependency only).

C++ anchors: `GameObject::Update` spawned-by-default respawn branch in `/home/server/woltk-trinity-legacy/src/server/game/Entities/GameObject/GameObject.cpp:1665-1672`, `Map::ApplyDynamicModeRespawnScaling` in `/home/server/woltk-trinity-legacy/src/server/game/Maps/Map.cpp:2242-2284`, declaration in `/home/server/woltk-trinity-legacy/src/server/game/Maps/Map.h:657-660`, and spawn-group dynamic-rate flag source in `/home/server/woltk-trinity-legacy/src/server/game/Maps/SpawnData.h` / Rust `SpawnGroupFlags::DYNAMIC_SPAWN_RATE`.

Implemented Rust dependency: `wow-map::map::apply_dynamic_mode_respawn_scaling_like_cpp` is a pure helper for `SpawnObjectType::GameObject` and `SpawnObjectType::Creature`. It preserves the C++ early returns for BG/arena maps, unsupported spawn types, missing spawn metadata, missing `DYNAMIC_SPAWN_RATE`, missing or zero zone-player count, `adjustFactor >= 1.0`, and `respawnDelay <= minimum`. Rust mode `0` and unsupported modes are safe no-ops instead of runtime panics. Successful scaling uses `ceil(respawnDelay * rate / playerCount)` clamped to the type-specific minimum, with separate creature vs gameobject rate/minimum config inputs.

Remaining gaps: this does not complete `#NEXT.R8.ENTITIES.021` and does not close GameObject respawn runtime. Still missing: wiring from canonical `GameObject::Update` / represented fallback into the helper, real `SaveRespawnTime`, canonical spawn metadata and zone-player-count ownership, map remove-list/cross-session ownership, visibility fanout, and persistence.

Implemented Rust dependency: `world-server` now builds a canonical in-memory `wow_map::SpawnStore` during startup after `spawn_group_template`, `Map.db2`, and `MapDifficulty.db2` are loaded. The loader reads `SEL_CREATURE_SPAWNS`, `SEL_GAMEOBJECT_SPAWNS`, `SEL_AREATRIGGER_SPAWNS`, and `SEL_SPAWN_GROUP_MEMBERS`; parses C++-shaped spawn difficulties; filters missing maps, invalid coordinates, and empty difficulty rows; reports represented/skipped validations; preserves game-event-managed Creature/GameObject rows as canonical `SpawnStore::spawns` metadata with `SpawnStore::insert_spawn_metadata_like_cpp` while omitting only their grid indexes, matching C++ `_creatureDataStore`/`_gameObjectDataStore` plus `gameEvent == 0` `AddCreatureToGrid`/`AddGameobjectToGrid`; indexes non-game-event Creature/GameObject rows with `SpawnStore::add_object_spawn`; indexes AreaTrigger rows with `add_area_trigger_spawn`; converts spawn-group templates through a minimal neutral iterator from `wow-data`; and applies member rows through `SpawnStore::apply_spawn_groups_like_cpp` rather than duplicating the assignment semantics in `world-server`. The startup log records row/index/skip counts and spawn-group assignment issues without wiring live grid/entity activation.

Remaining gaps: `ShouldBeSpawnedOnGridLoad` respawn/pool/group-active filter, live `ObjectGridLoader` wiring, canonical Creature/GameObject/AreaTrigger creation, respawn persistence, pool runtime, active spawn group state, canonical transport-map store for exact legacy default-group classification, full phase DB2 personal-phase lookup, VMAP invalid-position correction, AreaTrigger createProperties/spell validation, script-id resolution, and cross-session/runtime fanout remain pending.

### #NEXT.R8.ENTITIES.357 — Map-owned spawn-group active state bridge

Status: complete (Map-owned state bridge only; no live spawn/despawn/runtime activation).

It stops at the first future timer, deletes the entry before returning `UpdatePool`, deletes before returning `DoRespawn`, deletes on `check_respawn` false + zero, reinserts and returns `RescheduleAndSave` on false + future, and returns `InvalidRescheduleNotFuture` plus reinserts/breaks when a closure violates the C++ future-time assertion. `PoolMgr::UpdatePool`, `DoRespawn`, `SaveRespawnInfoDB`, `DeleteRespawnInfoFromDB`, linked-respawn checks, real by-spawn entity stores and LoadFromDB are intentionally not faked.
